_note: Causal chain definitions for third-order macro thinking. Each chain models a cause-effect sequence with lag times, probabilities, and score transmission factors.

chains.name: Tariff Escalation Chain
chains.description: Tariffs disrupt supply chains, raise input costs, compress margins, and eventually force earnings downgrades and sector rotation.
chains.totalTimelineDescription: Full chain: 3-9 months from tariff announcement to earnings impact

links.description: Tariffs disrupt established supply chains within 2-4 weeks
Supply chain disruption raises input costs within 4-8 weeks
Higher input costs compress corporate margins over 2-4 months
Compressed margins lead to earnings downgrades in 3-6 months
Downgrades trigger sector rotation within 5-15 days

chains.name: Credit Deterioration Chain
chains.description: Credit spreads widen → corporate bond stress → bank CDS rise → lending tightens → housing weakens → consumer spending drops. The classic 2007-2008 playbook.
chains.totalTimelineDescription: Full chain: 6-18 months from initial credit spread widening to consumer weakness

links.description: Credit spreads widening stresses corporate bond market within 1-3 weeks
Corporate bond stress raises bank CDS within 2-4 weeks
Bank stress leads to lending tightening within 1-3 months
Tighter lending weakens housing market within 2-6 months
Housing weakness hits consumer spending within 3-6 months

chains.name: Fed Policy Pivot Chain
chains.description: Hawkish Fed surprise steepens yield curve, strengthens dollar, stresses emerging markets, and weakens commodities.
chains.totalTimelineDescription: Full chain: 1-4 weeks from Fed surprise to commodity weakness

links.description: Fed hawkish surprise immediately moves yield curve
Yield curve move strengthens dollar within 1-3 days
Strong dollar stresses emerging markets within 1-4 weeks
Strong dollar weakens dollar-denominated commodities within 3-14 days

chains.name: Liquidity Crisis Chain
chains.description: Repo rate spike → dollar funding stress → credit freezing → forced deleveraging → contagion selling. The 2019 repo spike and 2008 credit freeze playbook.
chains.totalTimelineDescription: Full chain: 1-3 weeks from repo spike to contagion selling — FAST

links.description: Repo rate spike creates dollar funding stress within 1-3 days
Dollar funding stress freezes credit markets within 3-7 days
Frozen credit forces deleveraging within 1-5 days
Forced deleveraging triggers contagion selling within 0-2 days

chains.name: Bubble Pop Sequence
chains.description: Excessive leverage + narrow market leadership → vol spike → margin call cascade → forced selling → credit contagion → recession risk. The dot-com, 2008, and every bubble's endgame.
chains.totalTimelineDescription: Full chain: 2 weeks to 6 months from vol spike to recession risk

links.description: Excessive leverage makes markets fragile — any catalyst spikes vol within 0-7 days
Vol spike triggers margin calls within 1-3 days
Margin calls force selling within 0-2 days — reflexive loop
Forced selling widens credit spreads within 3-14 days
Credit contagion raises recession probability over 2-6 months

chains.name: Inflation Spiral Chain
chains.description: Hot inflation print → hawkish Fed expectations → rate hike fears → equity derating → growth scare. The 2022 playbook.
chains.totalTimelineDescription: Full chain: 1-6 months from hot print to growth scare

links.description: Hot CPI print immediately shifts Fed rate expectations
Hawkish expectations build rate hike fears within 1-5 days
Rate hike fears cause equity P/E compression within 5-30 days
Sustained equity weakness triggers growth scare narrative in 1-3 months
